headline: Ramana Maharshi: Fear of Death (1896)
description: At 16, Venkataraman extends his limbs and holds his breath, simulating rigor mortis, and investigates what remains when the body is 'dead.' The most compressed, precisely documented ego-dissolution in the spiritual record — over in minutes, irreversible. The self that was afraid did not survive the inquiry.

headline: Eckhart Tolle: The Night the Self Collapsed
description: A suicidally depressed Cambridge doctoral candidate wakes at 2am in a vortex of dread and notices a paradox: 'I cannot live with myself' implies two selves. He describes falling into a vortex of energy, then waking to birdsong as if hearing it for the first time. The next five months were characterized by uninterrupted peace.

headline: Adyashanti: Three Distinct Awakenings
description: Three events described with unusual precision: a kundalini surge at 25 that dissolved into infinite darkness; a nondual collapse of hearer and heard triggered by birdsong ('just one event, no separation'); and a final entry into 'absolute infinity of nothingness.' He notes that each felt complete, and each was followed by something further.

headline: Igor Kufayev: The Body on Fire During TM
description: During a Transcendental Meditation Siddhis course, Igor's skull splits with internal sound, his body hops involuntarily off the floor, and his chest opens 'from within, which physically was very painful.' He whispers to an administrator: 'This is who I really am.' What began that day never stopped.

headline: First Cessation: A Two-Year Meditation Journal
description: A contemporary practitioner documents the full arc — samatha jhanas, a 10-second no-self experience in August, and finally their first cessation: 'a missing moment, a small discontinuity.' What followed was 'a high-fidelity bubbling afterglow radiating out from my chest.' The account is notable for its precision and refusal to editorialize.

headline: Terence McKenna: True Hallucinations (La Chorrera, 1971)
description: McKenna's full account of the Amazon mushroom experiment that defined his life's work — the most phenomenologically detailed record of high-dose psilocybin in the literature. He describes the emergence of an 'I-Thou relationship' between the person and the mental state, the interior dialogue in light, and the encounter with what he could only call 'the other.'

headline: Jill Bolte Taylor: A Brain Scientist's Stroke from the Inside
description: A Harvard neuroanatomist narrates her left hemisphere shutting down in real time — and finding, in the expanding silence, an oceanic blissful state she recognized as what mystics describe. The most scientifically grounded account of boundary dissolution available. She had the vocabulary to watch it happen and the memory to report back.

headline: Psilocybin Mystical Experiences: What Participants Actually Said
description: 2022 study using natural language processing on raw session reports — no investigator-selected quotes. Participants describe: 'My body diffuses into rays of sand/light.' 'The light of love brings clarity to everything, connects everything like a network of roots.' The paper makes the phenomenology as rigorous as the neuroscience.

headline: Krishnamurti's Notebook: The Otherness (1961-62)
description: The only book Krishnamurti wrote himself — a daily journal recording something he couldn't name. He calls it 'the otherness,' 'the benediction,' 'the immensity.' It arrives without cause, departs without reason. 'There is a sense of being protected — not by something outside but by the very nature of what is happening.' The most sustained first-person record of a continuous mystical state in modern literature.

headline: Nisargadatta Maharaj: Dialogues from I Am That
description: From the Bombay beedi shop, 1970s. Nisargadatta describes consciousness observing itself, the 'I am' prior to all experience, without metaphysics or belief. His language is functional not devotional: 'The witness is not a person. The person is what's being witnessed.' These dialogues are among the most precise descriptions of nondual awareness in any tradition.
